zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Mortimer於1315年出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾維格莫時，她的父親Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March28歲，母親Joan de Geneville29歲。她於1330年6月13日之前與James Audley 2nd Baron Audley在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1351年12月之前在英國賀福爾維格莫。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Mortimer于1315年出生在英国英格兰賀福爾維格莫时，她的父亲Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March28岁，母亲Joan de Geneville29岁。她于1330年6月13日之前与James Audley 2nd Baron Audley在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1351年12月之前在英国賀福爾維格莫。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mortimer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Mortemer in Seine-Maritime, France, so called from Old French mort(e) ‘dead’ + mer ‘sea’, probably referring to a stagnant pond or drained swamp.

Irish (of Norman origin): the Norman surname in sense 1 above was taken to Ireland from England in the medieval period, where it has also been adopted by bearers of the Gaelic surnames Mac Muircheartaigh and ÓMuircheartaigh, commonly Anglicized as McMurty and Mortagh. Compare McMurdo .

variants.name: Mortimore
meanings: English (Devon; of Norman origin): variant of   Mortimer  .

variants.name: McMurdo
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muircheartaigh or Mac Muircheartaich, ‘son of Muircheartach’, a personal name meaning ‘sea-ruler’. In Scotland the name was often lenited to give McCurdy . Compare Murdoch and Moriarty . The name was also used to Anglicize Mac Murchaidh (see McMorrow ).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Mortimer was born in 1315, in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March, was 28 and her mother, Joan de Geneville, was 29. She married James Audley 2nd Baron Audley before 13 June 1330, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died before December 1351, in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Mortimer est née en 1315, à Wigmore, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Joan de Geneville, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé James Audley 2nd Baron Audley avant 13 juin 1330, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée avant décembre 1351, à Wigmore, Herefordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Mortimer 1315 in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March 28 und ihre Mutter Joan de Geneville 29. Sie hat vor 13. Juni 1330 in England James Audley 2nd Baron Audley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb vor Dezember 1351 in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Mortimer è nata il nel 1315 nel Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Joan de Geneville, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato James Audley 2nd Baron Audley prima del 13 giugno 1330, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì prima del dicembre 1351, nel Wigmore, Herefordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Mortimerが1315年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィグモーで生まれた時，父Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of Marchは28歳，母Joan de Genevilleは29歳であった。1330年6月13日以前にイギリスでJames Audley 2nd Baron Audleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1351年12月以前にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィグモーで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Mortimer(이)가 1315년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 위그모어에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan de Geneville(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1330년 6월 13일 이전 영국에서 James Audley 2nd Baron Audley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1351년 12월 이전 영국 허퍼드실르 위그모어에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Mortimer nasceu em 1315, em Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Geneville, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com James Audley 2nd Baron Audley antes 13 de junho de 1330, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu antes dezembro de 1351, em Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Mortimer родилась в 1315 г. в местности вигмор, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Geneville, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени James Audley 2nd Baron Audley перед 13 июнь 1330 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла перед декабрь 1351 г. в местности вигмор, хирефордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Mortimer nació en 1315, en Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Joan de Geneville, tenía 29 años. Se casó con James Audley 2nd Baron Audley antes de 13 de junio de 1330, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió antes de diciembre de 1351, en Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra.
